hazard.summary: Upstream 429 from ipapi.co propagated as a generic 500; no retry/backoff or Retry-After handling.
signals: ipapi.co responds with 429 Too Many Requests

constraints: 429 (and other retryable upstream failures) must not surface as generic 500 to callers
Retries must be bounded (max attempts) and must respect Retry-After when present
When upstream is rate-limiting, response must be classified as temporary (503) with Retry-After to shape client behavior
Fallback behavior must be explicit and non-null in production paths
verification: Induce 429 from ipapi.co (or stub upstream to 429) and confirm handler returns 503 + Retry-After (not 500).
Confirm retry attempts occur with exponential backoff and stop at UPSTREAM_MAX_RETRIES.
If Retry-After is present, confirm delay uses it instead of computed backoff.
Confirm production fallback path does not return null and does not re-emit a generic 500 for rate limits.
Run unit tests covering retry/backoff + exhausted retry classification.
notes: Issue was closed quickly via PR #134 and released; still receipt-worthy because it captures a durable pattern: upstream 429 should be retried with bounded backoff, Retry-After respected, and surfaced as 503 with Retry-After rather than a generic 500.
